full_name: Trial to Evaluate Treatment With Abbott Transcatheter Clip Repair System in Patients With Moderate or Greater TR (Single-Arm)
quick_summary: The single-arm feasibility study that established transcatheter edge-to-edge repair of the tricuspid valve as feasible and safe, reducing TR and improving symptoms — the basis for CE mark.
takeaway: Proved tricuspid TEER was feasible and safe, launching the modern T-TEER era.
population: Moderate or greater symptomatic TR, high surgical risk
primary_endpoint: Safety and reduction in TR severity.
key_results: Significant TR reduction with a strong safety profile
Durable symptom and functional improvement through 3 years
why_matters: First convincing tricuspid TEER dataset; led to CE mark (2020).
pearls: Used the first-generation clip in a highly selected population.
fda_impact: CE mark 2020; precursor to the US pivotal.

full_name: TRILUMINATE Pivotal Trial of Tricuspid TEER vs Medical Therapy
quick_summary: The first randomized tricuspid TEER trial: TriClip beat medical therapy on a hierarchical composite driven mainly by quality-of-life improvement, with excellent safety and durable TR reduction — the basis for FDA approval.
takeaway: Established tricuspid TEER as an FDA-approved therapy; benefit is real but largely symptomatic/QoL.
population: Severe symptomatic TR, intermediate-or-greater surgical risk
primary_endpoint: Hierarchical composite (win ratio) of death/tricuspid surgery, HF hospitalization, and KCCQ change at 1 year.
key_results: Win ratio 1.48 (95% CI 1.06–2.13, P=0.02), driven by quality of life
TR reduced to moderate-or-less in ~90% at 30 days, sustained at 1 year
>98% free from major adverse events at 30 days
No significant reduction in death or HF hospitalization
why_matters: Practice-changing: basis for FDA approval of TriClip (April 2024).
pearls: Benefit is predominantly symptomatic/QoL — counsel expectations accordingly.
TR reduction is durable and the procedure is remarkably safe.
limitations: Composite driven by KCCQ, not hard outcomes
guideline_esc: Supports TEER in selected severe TR.
fda_impact: TriClip FDA-approved April 2, 2024 (first tricuspid TEER); presented ACC.23.
authors: Sorajja P, Whisenant B, Hamid N, et al.

full_name: Multicentric Randomized Evaluation of Tricuspid TEER in Severe Secondary TR (Tri.Fr)
quick_summary: A French/Belgian investigator-led RCT: tricuspid TEER plus medical therapy improved a clinical composite (symptoms, TR, functional status) versus medical therapy alone — the second positive randomized T-TEER trial.
takeaway: Independent randomized confirmation that tricuspid TEER improves symptoms and functional status.
population: Severe isolated secondary TR, ineligible for surgery
primary_endpoint: Packer clinical composite score (improved / unchanged / worse) at 1 year.
key_results: Composite improvement 74.6% (TEER) vs 39.5% (GDMT); effect estimate 0.68 (P<0.0001)
Technical success 97.3%; in-hospital mortality 0.6%
Most patients required 2 devices
why_matters: Sponsor-independent confirmation of TRILUMINATE Pivotal's QoL/functional benefit.
pearls: Reinforces that the benefit is symptomatic/functional.

full_name: Real-World Outcomes of Tricuspid TEER With TriClip (bRIGHT Study)
quick_summary: A European post-market registry showing that tricuspid TEER reduces TR safely and durably across a broad range of real-world anatomies and centers.
takeaway: Real-world data confirm the safety and TR-reduction seen in the randomized trials.
population: Severe symptomatic TR in routine practice
key_results: Significant, durable TR reduction across broad anatomies
Safety consistent with the randomized trials
why_matters: Provides generalizability for the RCT findings.
pearls: Broader/less-selected patients and centers than TRILUMINATE.
authors: Lurz P, Rommel KP, Schmitz T, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
caveat: Post-market registry (not a dedicated NCT here).

full_name: Edwards PASCAL Tricuspid Repair System Early Feasibility Study
quick_summary: Early feasibility of the PASCAL system for tricuspid TEER, showing durable TR reduction and functional improvement — the PASCAL counterpart to the TriClip program.
takeaway: PASCAL is the second viable tricuspid TEER platform.
population: Severe symptomatic TR, high surgical risk
key_results: Durable TR reduction and functional improvement with good safety
why_matters: Feasibility basis for the PASCAL tricuspid pivotal (CLASP II TR).
pearls: Independent leaflet grasping and a central spacer suit large tricuspid gaps.
fda_impact: Precursor to CLASP II TR pivotal.
authors: Kodali S, Hahn RT, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: Edwards PASCAL Tricuspid Repair System Pivotal Trial
quick_summary: The pivotal randomized trial of PASCAL tricuspid TEER plus medical therapy versus medical therapy — the trial that would confirm a second TEER platform for approval.
takeaway: The key pivotal that could bring PASCAL to FDA approval for tricuspid TEER.
key_results: Pending — trial ongoing
why_matters: Would establish PASCAL as an approved T-TEER option.
pearls: Watch how PASCAL vs TriClip outcomes compare in tricuspid anatomy.

full_name: Edwards EVOQUE Tricuspid Valve Replacement Early Feasibility Study
quick_summary: The feasibility study of transfemoral EVOQUE tricuspid replacement showed near-elimination of TR with strong functional gains — establishing dedicated TTVR as viable.
takeaway: EVOQUE showed that transcatheter tricuspid replacement can nearly abolish TR.
population: Severe symptomatic TR, high surgical risk
key_results: ~98% achieved TR ≤mild
Marked functional and quality-of-life improvement
Pacemaker implantation and bleeding are the main trade-offs
why_matters: Established dedicated TTVR; basis for the TRISCEND II pivotal.
pearls: Replacement abolishes TR more completely than repair — at the cost of anticoagulation/pacemakers.
fda_impact: Breakthrough-device program; precursor to TRISCEND II.
authors: Kodali S, Hahn RT, et al.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology

full_name: Edwards EVOQUE Tricuspid Valve Replacement Pivotal Trial
quick_summary: The first randomized TTVR trial: EVOQUE plus medical therapy was superior to medical therapy on a hierarchical composite (win ratio ~2.0), with near-complete TR elimination — basis for the first FDA-approved tricuspid replacement.
takeaway: EVOQUE is the first FDA-approved tricuspid replacement, with the strongest win ratio in the field.
primary_endpoint: Hierarchical composite (win ratio) of death, RVAD/transplant, tricuspid intervention, HF hospitalization, KCCQ, NYHA, and 6-minute walk at 1 year.
key_results: Win ratio 2.02 (95% CI 1.56–2.62, P<0.001) favoring EVOQUE
~95–99% achieved TR ≤mild
More new pacemakers and bleeding with EVOQUE
why_matters: Basis for the first FDA-approved transcatheter tricuspid replacement (Feb 2024).
pearls: Largest treatment effect in tricuspid trials — but weigh pacemaker/anticoagulation trade-offs.
Approved without an FDA advisory panel, on a prespecified 6-month analysis of the first 150 patients.
guideline_esc: EVOQUE CE mark (2023); supports replacement in selected severe TR.
fda_impact: EVOQUE FDA-approved February 2024 (first transcatheter tricuspid replacement); TCT 2023/2024.
authors: Hahn RT, Makkar R, Kodali S, et al.

full_name: LuX-Valve / LuX-Valve Plus Transcatheter Tricuspid Replacement Program
quick_summary: A non-radial-force tricuspid replacement anchored to the interventricular septum; early (mainly Chinese) experience shows high TR elimination, with transfemoral (LuX-Valve Plus) iterations advancing.
takeaway: A distinct TTVR design worth tracking as it moves toward transfemoral delivery and wider study.
population: Severe symptomatic TR, high surgical risk
key_results: High TR elimination in early series; broader/Western data still limited
why_matters: Septal-anchoring design avoids radial force on the annulus.
pearls: LuX-Valve Plus moves to transjugular/transfemoral delivery.
caveat: Primarily Chinese experience; Western pivotal data limited. NCT not confirmed here.

full_name: TrIcuspid Regurgitation RePAIr With Cardioband Device Study
quick_summary: Direct transcatheter annuloplasty of the tricuspid annulus with Cardioband reduced annular size and TR with durable symptom improvement — a repair-based alternative to TEER and replacement.
takeaway: Direct annuloplasty can remodel the tricuspid annulus, but the evidence base is smaller than TEER.
population: Symptomatic functional TR, high surgical risk
key_results: Significant annular reduction and TR improvement
Durable functional gains through 2 years
why_matters: CE-mark tricuspid annuloplasty option; leaflet- and anatomy-preserving.
pearls: Right coronary artery course is a key procedural consideration.

full_name: Percutaneous Tricuspid Valve Annuloplasty System for Symptomatic Chronic Functional TR (Trialign)
quick_summary: An early feasibility study of the Trialign system, which plicates the annulus to mimic a surgical Kay bicuspidization, showing reduced annular area and TR.
takeaway: An early proof-of-concept for suture-based tricuspid annuloplasty.
key_results: Reduced annular area and TR with functional improvement
Single-leaflet dehiscence observed in some patients
why_matters: One of the first transcatheter tricuspid annuloplasty concepts.
pearls: Recreates a surgical Kay-type annuloplasty percutaneously.
journal: Journal of the American College of Cardiology
caveat: Early device; limited ongoing clinical development.

full_name: Safety and Efficacy of the TricValve Transcatheter Bicaval Valves System (TRICUS EURO)
quick_summary: A dedicated bicaval valve system (SVC + IVC) that treats the congestive consequences of severe TR rather than the valve itself; improved symptoms and quality of life at 6–12 months in high-risk patients — the basis for CE mark.
takeaway: Caval valve implantation palliates congestion when the valve itself can't be repaired or replaced.
population: Severe symptomatic TR, high surgical risk, significant caval backflow
key_results: Significant quality-of-life and functional improvement at 6–12 months
Does not reduce true tricuspid TR — treats caval backflow/congestion
Mortality aligned with baseline TRI-SCORE
why_matters: Offers a palliative option for patients unsuitable for TEER or orthotopic replacement.
pearls: Treats symptoms of congestion, not the regurgitation — set expectations accordingly.
Useful when coaptation gaps or anatomy preclude TEER/replacement.

full_name: Heterotopic Implantation of the SAPIEN Valve in the Inferior Vena Cava for Severe TR
quick_summary: A feasibility study implanting a balloon-expandable SAPIEN valve in the inferior vena cava to palliate right-heart congestion from severe TR in inoperable patients.
takeaway: Proof-of-concept that an off-the-shelf valve in the IVC can palliate TR-related congestion.
population: Severe TR, inoperable / very high risk
key_results: Feasible IVC implantation with symptom palliation in selected patients
why_matters: Early demonstration of single-site caval valve implantation.
pearls: Palliative — addresses congestion, not the tricuspid valve.
caveat: Off-label use of a commercially available valve; very small feasibility study.

full_name: European Feasibility Study of the CroíValve DUO Transcatheter Tricuspid Coaptation Valve System
quick_summary: An early feasibility study of a coaptation-valve system that anchors in the SVC and places a spacer at the tricuspid annulus — a hybrid concept between caval and orthotopic approaches.
takeaway: A novel coaptation-valve concept to watch in the crowded tricuspid frontier.
key_results: Pending — early feasibility ongoing
why_matters: Represents the diversity of next-generation tricuspid approaches.
pearls: Combines a caval anchor with an annular coaptation element.
